next_action: Launch a multi-branch portfolio targeting class coverage (primary) and pseudo-label (novel), with a hedged prior_calibration branch conditional on coverage fix.
root_cause: The probe model is the primary bottleneck: train soundscape ROC-AUC (0.992) vs val (0.665) reveals severe overfitting or train/val distribution gap. Only 52/75 classes are fitted (31% coverage deficit), which structurally blocks submission bar criteria. The Bayesian prior is paradoxically stronger on validation (0.662) than train (0.487), confirming the probe is the weak link, not the prior.
why: The class coverage deficit (23/75 unfitted classes) is the single highest-priority structural blocker with positive policy support (prefer). The veto checker forbids probe_head, the branch_typing_compiler flags calibration_only/blend_only as low-information, and exp-0010 definitively killed naive blend-ratio tuning. The branch diversifier recommends class_coverage (primary) + prior_calibration (hedge). The novel hypothesis generator suggests pseudo_label as an unexplored axis. Collapsing to a single calibration tweak would violate the decision rules requiring multi-branch search when below target.
next_title: Class coverage fix — resolve 23 unfitted classes
portfolio_intent: Multi-branch search targeting the class coverage structural blocker (primary), lighter regularization that avoids forbidden probe_head patterns (hedge), and pseudo-labeling as a novel data-axis intervention. All branches must respect the probe_head veto and low-information pattern guards.

branch_selection_rules: hedge must be conditional on coverage fix and avoid low-information patterns
novel must target pseudo_label or another unsupported axis with explicit evidence requirements
no branch may target probe_head without explicit override justification
portfolio_policy.submission_bar: Round-1 baseline; hold until 4+ rounds with multiple techniques per 04_submission_bar.md

branch_mix.rationale: 31% coverage deficit is the single blocking issue with positive policy support
Label smoothing may reduce overfitting without gradient suppression; conditional on coverage fix
Bayesian prior provides high-confidence predictions usable as pseudo-labels for unfitted classes

branch_portfolio.title: Class coverage fix — resolve 23 unfitted classes
branch_portfolio.hypothesis: Fixing the 23 unfitted classes will raise val AUC above 0.68 by closing the class coverage gap.
branch_portfolio.rationale: Strongest policy support (prefer class_coverage). 31% coverage deficit is the structural blocker. All downstream work depends on full class coverage.
branch_portfolio.falsification_criterion: Val AUC does not improve when all 75 classes are fitted
branch_portfolio.kill_criterion: After fixing coverage, val AUC remains below 0.66

branch_portfolio.title: Label smoothing regularization on probe logits
branch_portfolio.hypothesis: Label smoothing (epsilon 0.05-0.1) will reduce train/val overfitting without suppressing gradient signal on the small training set.
branch_portfolio.rationale: Conditional prior_calibration policy. Exp-0011 showed dropout kills gradient signal; label smoothing is lighter. Must avoid calibration_only low-information pattern by pairing with coverage fix.
branch_portfolio.override_reason: Label smoothing targets overfitting via loss function, not probe architecture; distinct from vetoed probe_head pattern
branch_portfolio.falsification_criterion: Label smoothing does not reduce train/val AUC gap
branch_portfolio.kill_criterion: Train/val gap remains above 0.25 after label smoothing

branch_portfolio.title: Pseudo-label unfitted classes from Bayesian prior
branch_portfolio.hypothesis: Pseudo-labeling from high-confidence Bayesian prior predictions on unfitted classes may improve coverage and discriminative power simultaneously.
branch_portfolio.rationale: Novel hypothesis generator suggests pseudo_label as unexplored axis. Bayesian prior val AUC (0.662) provides usable signal. Addresses coverage gap from data perspective rather than architecture.
unsupported_claims: pseudo_label may unlock holdout improvements despite limited direct support
required_evidence: Overall val AUC improves vs baseline 0.665
branch_portfolio.falsification_criterion: Pseudo-labeled classes do not achieve above-random predictions
branch_portfolio.kill_criterion: Pseudo-labeling produces no class coverage improvement or degrades existing class performance

hypothesis_backlog.hypothesis: Fixing the 23 unfitted classes will raise val AUC above 0.68 by closing the class coverage gap.
Label smoothing (epsilon 0.05-0.1) will reduce train/val overfitting without suppressing gradient signal.
Pseudo-labeling from Bayesian prior predictions will improve coverage for unfitted classes.
The frozen Perch embedding space is a hard ceiling and a different backbone is needed for significant AUC gains.

open_questions: Will fixing the 23 unfitted classes alone close the train/val AUC gap, or does the probe architecture itself need fundamental change?
Is the frozen Perch embedding space a hard ceiling for this lane, as suggested by the 2/11 success rate in exp-0011?
Can pseudo-labeling from the Bayesian prior produce reliable labels for the 23 unfitted classes?

active_veto_rules.summary: probe_head has stronger contradictory evidence than supporting evidence.

unsupported_claims: pseudo_label may unlock holdout improvements despite limited direct support.
required_evidence: Test an unsupported but plausible pseudo_label hypothesis with explicit follow-up evidence.

problem_frame.root_cause: The probe model is the primary bottleneck: train soundscape ROC-AUC (0.992) vs val (0.665) reveals severe overfitting. Only 52/75 classes are fitted (31% coverage deficit).
